title: New Hampshire Federal Contracts: $1.1B in FY2026
h1: Federal Contracts in New Hampshire: Spending, Agencies & Vendors
metaDescription: Federal agencies obligated $1.1B to contractors in New Hampshire in FY2026 to date. See top buying agencies, top vendors, and industries.

charts.title: Obligations by fiscal year
charts.takeaway: Obligations fell 62.0% year over year, from $2.9B in FY2025 to $1.1B in FY2026 to date.

narrative.intro: Federal contract obligations to New Hampshire in FY2026 to date totaled $1.1B across 2,930 awards, with an average award size of $378K. Obligations fell 62.0% year over year. This fiscal-year-to-date total is partial and reflects activity through the current fiscal period. The Department of Defense accounted for the largest share of buying-agency obligations at $960M, far exceeding the next agencies: Department of Veterans Affairs at $24.4M and Department of Justice at $19.6M. The state’s top vendors to date include BAE SYSTEMS INFORMATION AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS INTEGRATION INC. at $559M and SIG SAUER INC. at $123M. Industry concentration is dominated by aerospace and defense-related NAICS categories, led by Other Aircraft Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing at $198M and Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing at $157M. Historical annual obligations show FY24 and FY25 at $2.9B each, compared with FY26 at $1.1B to date, and prior years ranging from $1.7B to $1.9B. These figures reflect obligations only and current-year totals are partial (fiscal year to date).

sections.heading: How concentrated is federal buying in New Hampshire?
sections.body: Buying is highly concentrated: the Department of Defense obligated $960M in FY2026 to date, representing the dominant single-agency buyer. The next agencies are substantially smaller buyers, with Department of Veterans Affairs at $24.4M and Department of Justice at $19.6M, indicating a strong DoD focus in the state’s federal market.

sections.heading: Who captures the largest share of obligations in New Hampshire?
sections.body: A small set of vendors capture the largest shares: BAE SYSTEMS INFORMATION AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS INTEGRATION INC. received $559M and SIG SAUER INC. received $123M in FY2026 to date. Industry spending is concentrated in aerospace and defense-related NAICS categories, led by Other Aircraft Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing at $198M and Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing at $157M.

faq.q: How much federal contract money goes to New Hampshire?
faq.a: New Hampshire received $1.1B in federal contract obligations in FY2026 to date. Total obligations are spread across 2,930 awards with an average award size of $378K, and obligations fell 62.0% year over year. FY24 and FY25 each show $2.9B in obligations for historical comparison.

faq.q: Which agencies award the most contracts in New Hampshire?
faq.a: The Department of Defense is the largest contracting agency in New Hampshire, obligating $960M in FY2026 to date. The next largest buyers are Department of Veterans Affairs at $24.4M and Department of Justice at $19.6M.

faq.q: Who are the largest federal contractors in New Hampshire?
faq.a: BAE SYSTEMS INFORMATION AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS INTEGRATION INC. is the largest federal contractor in New Hampshire at $559M in FY2026 to date. SIG SAUER INC. is the second-largest at $123M, followed by ENVISION TECHNOLOGY, LLC at $70.8M.
